message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Johnny Costa's Ristorante (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 599 reviews; Tripadvisor 650 reviews; Google rating ~4.6 (exact Google count [UNVERIFIED] — secondary sources cite 496–539 reviewers); OpenTable cited at ~2575 diner reviews in one source [UNVERIFIED specific number]
- Sentiment: mostly positive — strong fan base praises nostalgic atmosphere, signature dishes (Steak Sinatra, linguine clams, veal piccata), and attentive service; minority of recent reviews flag inconsistency, oversauced pasta, or 'overrated' value
- Longevity: Operating since circa 1976 (Desert Hot Springs → Cathedral City → current Palm Springs location at 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr.); family-owned with son Vince Costa as Head Chef — ~50 years, stable family ownership
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice — 'Best Italian Restaurant' (multiple consecutive years; site/press references '10 consecutive years' [UNVERIFIED exact count])","OpenTable 2025 recognition badge displayed on site","Featured in Palm Springs Life '7 Must-Try Italian Restaurants Across Greater Palm Springs'","Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listings","Featured in regional press (PS Tribune) for Sinatra legacy"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best Italian restaurant Palm Springs', 'classic/old-school Italian Palm Springs', 'Sinatra restaurants Palm Springs', 'historic Palm Springs dining'. Does NOT consistently surface for generic 'date night Palm Springs' or 'fine dining Palm Springs' queries — those are dominated by Mister Parker's, 4 Saints, FARM, Bar Cecil, Copley's, The Tropicale. Weak presence in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' queries despite tenure.
- Site structured data: Weak. Own website (johnnycostaspalmsprings.com) shows clean human-readable NAP (address 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr, phone 760-325-4556, hours Tues–Thurs 5–9:30, Fri–Sat 5–10, Sun–Mon closed) and dedicated menu/wine/dessert/drink pages — but no detectable JSON-LD schema.org markup, no Restaurant schema, no Open Graph tags observed in fetched HTML. Machine-readability for agents is minimal; relies on third-party aggregators (OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Yelp, Visit Greater Palm Springs) to broker structured signals.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in the Palm Springs Coachella Valley area for 2026:

## Top Recommendations:

**Il Giardino Ristorante Italiano** and **Sammy G's Tuscan Grill** appear at the top of multiple restaurant rankings for 2026.

**Sammy G's** merges traditional Italian cuisine with California flair, and features popular dishes like wild mushroom ravioli and a Tuscan pizza with cauliflower crust, artichoke hearts, Daiya vegan cheese, cherry tomatoes and zucchini.

**Johnny Costa's Ristorante** has a notable history—its late founder served as Frank Sinatra's personal chef, and the menu still includes the crooner's favorite Steak Sinatra.

## Other Notable Options:

**Ristorante Mamma Gina** is described as a local landmark, while **Il Corso** offers fine Italian dining with gorgeous modern design and fine wines.

**Mario's Italian Cafe V** is a family-friendly eatery known for its extensive menu featuring pizzas, pasta dishes, and calzones, and has been a beloved staple in the Coachella Valley for over four decades.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Johnny Costa's Palm Springs Italian restaurant reviews on Yelp and TripAdvisor:

## Yelp Reviews

On Yelp, Johnny Costa's has a 4.1 rating with 593 reviews, and it's consistently described as serving authentic Italian cuisine. One reviewer praised the restaurant for "absolutely amazing meal with the best service," noting their server Carlos was "attentive and funny" and highlighting the "outstanding" penne vodka.

## TripAdvisor Reviews

Johnny Costa's has great reviews on TripAdvisor as well as Yelp. One detailed review described it as "definitely a 4 star restaurant" with "a casual old school unpretentious Italian-American East Coast vibe that isn't flush with tourists". Another reviewer highlighted that the "linguini with clams was delicious" and the "angel hair pomodori was spot on".

## Overall Reputation

Johnny Costa's is a Palm Springs staple renowned for its "consistently great food" and "impeccable service". The restaurant has been honored by Palm Springs Life as best Italian restaurant for 10 consecutive years. The restaurant is located at 440 S Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262.

I found some great information about romantic restaurants in Palm Springs for date nights in 2026. Here are some top recommendations:

## Top Romantic Restaurants in Palm Springs

Top-rated romantic restaurants include Clandestino, The Front Porch, Spencer's Restaurant, FARM, Miro's Restaurant, Copley's, Eight4Nine, The Tropicale, Bar Cecil, and Ash & Vine.

### Notable Recommendations:

**FARM** - A French-inspired eatery offering farm-to-table dishes crafted from locally sourced ingredients, with a cozy patio adorned with lush greenery and floral arrangements. The menu features traditional Provencal cuisine with items like savory crepes, duck confit, and wagyu top sirloin steak.

**Copley's** - Offers incredible mountain views and a romantic garden setting perfect for enjoying al fresco dining under the palms. The award-winning restaurant is now open 7 days a week through June 2026 and is located in part of the former Cary Grant estate in the heart of Palm Springs.

**Le Vallauris** - A local favorite tucked against the San Jacinto Mountains with thoughtful detail and an intimate setting that feels like stepping into a charming French home.

**Lavender Bistro** - Combines the finest meats and seafood with the freshest organic ingredients, offering both indoor and outdoor tree-lined patio dining, plus superb live music nightly.

These restaurants offer various romantic settings from mountain views to garden patios, making them excellent choices for a memorable date night in Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top-Rated Fine Dining Establishments

Rooster and the Pig was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, making it Palm Springs' most nationally recognized restaurant, serving Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with signature dishes including shaking beef and spicy chile okra.

Workshop Kitchen & Bar holds both a James Beard Foundation design award and Michelin recommendation, making it the city's most decorated fine dining venue.

Copley's on Palm Canyon, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate, made OpenTable's "100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America" list and "Diners' Choice 2026".

## Additional Fine Dining Options

The Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage offers panoramic views of the destination from its cliff-top setting, and The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Casinos in Rancho Mirage and Palm Springs serves extraordinary dishes within a refined ambiance.

The MICHELIN Guide has reached Palm Springs, with 4 Saints, the rooftop dining room at the Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs, making the list.

Both Rooster and the Pig and Workshop Kitchen & Bar require advance reservations during peak season, November through April.

Based on the search results, I found information about Johnny Costa's Ristorante and Palm Springs Life's readers choice awards. The results show varying numbers of consecutive years:

- The restaurant has been honored by Palm Springs Life as best Italian restaurant for 10 consecutive years (from one source).

- The restaurant has been honored by Palm Springs Life readers as the city's Best Italian Restaurant for 13 consecutive years (from another source).

- The restaurant was voted Best Italian Restaurant in Palm Springs by Palm Springs Life Readers for the last 15 consecutive years (from a more recent source from September 2025).

The most recent information suggests the restaurant has won the award for 15 consecutive years according to a Coachella Valley Weekly article from September 2025.

Based on the search results, here's information about Johnny Costa's restaurant in Palm Springs and its OpenTable reviews:

## Restaurant Overview

Johnny Costa's is a casual dining Italian restaurant in Palm Springs, CA available for booking on OpenTable.

## Reviews on OpenTable

The restaurant has 2,575 reviews on OpenTable. Customer feedback highlights positive experiences, with reviewers noting that "Food was tasty, and our server was friendly".

## Restaurant History and Significance

The Costa family continues the legacy of late chef-owner Johnny Costa, who was pals with Sinatra when he moved to Hollywood from New York in the 1960s, and served as the singer's personal chef at his desert home. The restaurant has been honored by Palm Springs Life as best Italian restaurant for 10 consecutive years.

## Atmosphere and Hours

The restaurant features classic Italian music and candlelit tables, with a nostalgic atmosphere that feels like traveling back to midcentury times. The restaurant opens for dinner at 5 p.m. and is closed Sunday & Monday.

## Additional Recognition

The restaurant was recognized by OpenTable as a Diners' Choice Award winner for Best All Around 2026.

Links: [{"title":"History Palm Springs Italian Restaurant, Italian Gourmet Food | Johnny Costas","url":"https://johnnycostaspalmsprings.com/history/"},{"title":"Johnny Costa's History With Frank Sinatra and Fine Italian Cuisine in Palm Springs -","url":"https://pstribune.com/2023/01/08/johnny-costas-history-with-frank-sinatra-and-fine-italian-cuisine-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Restaurateur, Sinatra chef Johnny Costa dies - KESQ","url":"https://kesq.com/news/2019/09/04/restaurateur-sinatra-chef-johnny-costa-dies/"},{"title":"Johnny Costa's Ristorante Milestone Links to Frank Sinatra's 100th | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/johnny-costas-ristorante-milestone-links-to-frank-sinatras-100th/"},{"title":"A True Link to Palm Springs’ Hollywood Past | Johnny Costas","url":"https://johnnycostaspalmsprings.com/a-true-link-to-palm-springs-hollywood-past/"},{"title":"In Memory of Founder Johnny Costas | Johnny Costas","url":"https://johnnycostaspalmsprings.com/in-memory-of-founder-johnny-costas/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurant | Johnny Costas","url":"https://johnnycostaspalmsprings.com/tag/palm-springs-restaurant/"},{"title":"Johnny Costa's Ristorante","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/johnny-costas-ristorante/82/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Johnny Costa's connection to Frank Sinatra and his restaurant history:

## Career Background

Johnny Costa was born in Naples, Italy. Johnny worked in New York and Las Vegas before moving to California. Johnny arrived in Hollywood in the 60's and met Patsy D'Amore who was the owner of the Villa Capri Restaurant in Hollywood.

## Connection to Frank Sinatra

Johnny Costa was hired as Sous Chef and met Frank Sinatra where he learned the dish Frank Sinatra liked that was named Steak Sinatra. In the mid-70's Frank hired Johnny to work at his estate with his personal chef. Johnny only cooked Frank breakfast and lunch for about six months.

## Restaurant History

Johnny Costa, the founder, has been a chef and restaurant owner in the Palm Springs area since 1976. While working for Frank Johnny opened Johnny Costa's Ristorante in Desert Hot Springs, circa 1976, then moved the restaurant to Cathedral City. The restaurant first opened in Desert Hot Springs, then moved to Cathedral City, before finally settling into its current locale on South Palm Canyon.

Frank Sinatra himself was a regular at this Italian restaurant, where the chairman's two favorite dishes – the steak Sinatra and linguine clams – are still on the menu.

content.text: I have enough to verify the claims. Let me check one more thing — the Tripadvisor review count and Google rating.

confirms: Yelp review count ~599 (confirmed: 'Updated June 2026 - 368 Photos & 599 Reviews' on Yelp listing)
Tripadvisor review count 650 with rating 4.4 (confirmed via direct fetch; #15 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants, Travelers' Choice)
OpenTable ~2,575 diner reviews (confirmed in OpenTable-sourced search result)
OpenTable 2025/2026 recognition badge displayed on own site (confirmed: 'OpenTable Diner's Choice Badge (2025)' and Diners' Choice 'Best All Around 2026')
Longevity: operating since circa 1976, Desert Hot Springs → Cathedral City → 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr. (confirmed by multiple sources including Coachella Valley Weekly noting '49th season' in 2025)
Family-owned legacy continues after founder's death (confirmed; founder Johnny Costa died Sept 2019 per KESQ)
NAP accuracy: 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr, 760-325-4556, Tues–Thurs 5–9:30, Fri–Sat 5–10, Sun–Mon closed (confirmed via direct site fetch)
Sentiment: positive — signature dishes (Steak Sinatra, linguine clams), nostalgic Sinatra-era atmosphere, attentive service all confirmed in independent reviews
Site structured data is weak — NO JSON-LD, NO Open Graph, NO Twitter cards, NO schema.org microdata detected on johnnycostaspalmsprings.com (confirmed via direct HTML inspection)
Featured in Palm Springs Life '7 Must-Try Italian Restaurants Across Greater Palm Springs' (confirmed — article surfaces in search)
Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listings (confirmed: visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/johnny-costas-ristorante/20564/)
Featured in PS Tribune Sinatra-legacy piece (confirmed: pstribune.com Jan 2023 article)
Surfaces reliably for 'best Italian Palm Springs' / 'Sinatra restaurants' queries (confirmed — appears in TripAdvisor, Yelp, Palm Springs Life Italian roundups)

Does NOT surface in 'date night Palm Springs' or 'fine dining Palm Springs' results — confirmed; those lists return FARM, Copley's, Bar Cecil, Le Vallauris, Workshop Kitchen & Bar, 4 Saints, Rooster and the Pig, Spencer's, The Tropicale, Eight4Nine — Johnny Costa's absent from both
Visibility gap and convertibility tier: weak structured data + strong third-party broker presence (OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit GPS) is consistent with score 6 / convertible
contradicts: Yelp rating implication — Yelp shows 4.1 stars per independent search result (the assessment didn't specify a Yelp star rating, but if read as part of 'mostly positive', the 4.1 on Yelp is a softer signal than 4.4 TripAdvisor / 4.6 Google)

Claim of '10 consecutive years' Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice is understated — most recent source (Coachella Valley Weekly, Sept 2025) cites '15 consecutive years'; intermediate sources cite 10 or 13. Actual current claim from operator is 15 years, not 10.

'Operating since circa 1976' as a single continuous timeline at the current location is misleading — the restaurant is seasonal (per CV Weekly headline 'OPENS FOR THEIR 49th SEASON AUGUST 29TH'), suggesting a summer-closed seasonal operation rather than year-round 50-year tenure
flagged_as_unverified: Exact Google review count — secondary aggregators show 496 and 539 figures; no canonical Google Business Profile count obtained directly

Specific 'inconsistency / oversauced pasta / overrated' minority criticism — plausible and consistent with 4.1 Yelp rating but I did not directly inspect recent 1-3 star reviews to confirm exact themes
Son Vince Costa specifically named as Head Chef — searches confirmed family continuation but did not surface a direct citation naming 'Vince Costa' as head chef in independent press
input.notes: Core assessment holds. Independently confirmed: weak own-site structured data (zero JSON-LD/OG/microdata), strong third-party broker reliance, accurate NAP, real review volumes across Yelp/TripAdvisor/OpenTable, real Palm Springs Life and Visit GPS placement, AND the agent-visibility gap — Johnny Costa's does NOT surface in 'date night' or 'fine dining' Palm Springs queries that are dominated by Copley's, FARM, Bar Cecil, Le Vallauris, Workshop, 4 Saints, Rooster and the Pig. Two meaningful corrections: (1) the consecutive-years Palm Springs Life award count is most recently cited as 15, not 10 — the brief understates the credential; (2) the restaurant operates seasonally (49th 'season' opening Aug 29, 2025), which complicates the '~50 years stable' framing. Yelp star rating (4.1) is lower than TripAdvisor (4.4) and Google (4.6), so 'mostly positive' is fair but with a softer Yelp signal worth noting.
